untranslatable_reason: The Bulkowski measure rule requires manual identification of three specific price points (A: prior minor high, B: formation low at start, C: high at formation end) and calculation of: (A - B) subtracted from C. These are chart pattern landmarks that cannot be algorithmically identified without pattern recognition logic beyond the allowed indicators. The rule calculates: Target = C - (A - B), which requires marked price points that must be identified by visual inspection or dedicated pattern recognition, not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: Entry condition uses a proxy (close breaking below previous low) to approximate downside breakout confirmation. Exit uses pivot support (s1) as a proxy for target calculation, but actual measure rule calculation cannot be implemented with allowed indicators. Manual point identification required before backtesting.
